title: Sales supervisors - retail and wholesale
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate the operations of public or private sector organizations, overseeing multiple departments or locations. Duties and responsibilities include formulating policies, managing daily operations, and planning the use of materials and human resources, but are too diverse and general in nature to be classified in any one functional area of management or administration, such as personnel, purchasing, or administrative services. Usually manage through subordinate supervisors. Excludes First-Line Supervisors. Directly supervise and coordinate activities of retail sales workers in an establishment or department. Duties may include management functions, such as purchasing, budgeting, accounting, and personnel work, in addition to supervisory duties. Solicit donations or orders for goods or services over the telephone.

tasks.task: Provide customer service by greeting and assisting customers, responding to inquiries and complaints, and contacting businesses or individuals to solicit sales or donations.
Instruct staff on handling difficult and complicated sales, enforce safety, health, and security rules, and assign specific duties to employees.
Monitor sales activities and suppliers to ensure satisfactory service, quality goods, and efficient operations.
Establish credit policies, formulate pricing policies based on profitability, and review financial statements to measure productivity and identify areas for improvement.
Confer with company officials to develop methods to increase sales, prepare budgets, and authorize payments and returns.
Plan and coordinate advertising campaigns, promotions, and merchandise displays to enhance customer engagement.
Review inventory and sales records, maintain records of purchases, sales, and other transactions, and keep updated records of employee work schedules and time cards.
Estimate consumer demand and determine types and amounts of goods to be sold, and direct and coordinate activities related to production and distribution of products.
Hire, train, and evaluate personnel in sales, performing necessary personnel functions such as promotion or termination when appropriate.
Examine merchandise to ensure proper pricing and display, assess received products for resale, and perform floor work related to sales and inventory management.
Record names, addresses, purchases, and reactions of prospects contacted, and obtain customer information to process orders.
Deliver sales presentations using prepared scripts, making adjustments to better target specific individual needs, and follow up sales contacts through telephone or correspondence.
